title: tachigali paraensis
intro: Tachigali paraensis, also known as the Para tachigal or Pau Brasil, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The medicinal properties of Tachigali paraensis are attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and prevent certain types of cancer, among others. Tachigali paraensis is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves and bark in hot water, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a paste or powder.
